title: [Docs] Full podman uninstall commands
body:
### Feature request description

It is common for users to want to change Podman versions for testing or rolling back from an upgrade. These users may encounter issues and need to do a fresh install of Podman. Other user want to remove everything an app like Podman installs on their computer. Currently, there are no documented commands or scripts that achieve this.

### Suggest potential solution

A tutorial is added to [Podman.io tutorials](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/Tutorials.html) with separate commands for Mac, Windows, and Linux. We could turn this commands into a script which could also be used in [Podman Desktop](https://podman-desktop.io/).

I've started on a basic uninstall for Mac and Windows (delete `/containers` files & folders and call OS uninstall command) here https://github.com/NiceNode/nice-node/tree/feat/podman-runtime/src/main/podman/uninstall

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Not aware of any

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

@vrothberg please let me know if you have any suggestions or guidelines and then I can get started.

@ashley-cui @n1hility do the above folders look right?

But going one step back.  I think Podman should do `system reset` and remove these files when being uninstalled on Win and Mac.
The Mac folders LGTM.
looks good there is one other place for Windows, container.conf is in `AppData\Roaming\containers\containers.conf`
(BTW I agree on system reset. The only issue would be if there are running vms around for running machine instances it would need to kill them all first or error)

Mac command to kill the qemu process(es): `kill $(ps aux | grep qemu | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}')`

Reference Stack overflow: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/22334761/how-to-kill-all-processes-with-the-same-name-using-os-x-terminal

> (BTW I agree on system reset. The only issue would be if there are running vms around for running machine instances it would need to kill them all first or error)

> @ashley-cui @n1hility do the above folders look right?
> 
> But going one step back. I think Podman should do `system reset` and remove these files when being uninstalled on Win and Mac.

Were you suggesting that this command should also run `podman system reset`? Just want to make sure you didn't mean that this command should be included in `system reset`
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@n1hility @vrothberg @ashley-cui waiting for  you I believe?
As a user looking how to completely remove podman from macOS, I think such uninstall guideline is a **must**.

@jgresham I think you list from Mac is missing:

* ~/Library/Application Support/Podman Desktop/
* ~/Library/Application Support/Caches/podman-desktop-updater/
* ~/Library/Saved Application State/io.podmandesktop.PodmanDesktop.savedState/
* ~/Library/Preferences/io.podmandesktop.PodmanDesktop.plist
* ~/Library/Preferences/ByHost/io.podmandesktop.PodmanDesktop.ShipIt*
* ~/Library/Logs/Podman Desktop

> @jgresham I think you list from Mac is missing:
> 
> * ~/Library/Application Support/Podman Desktop/
> * ~/Library/Application Support/Caches/podman-desktop-updater/
> * ~/Library/Saved Application State/io.podmandesktop.PodmanDesktop.savedState/
> * ~/Library/Preferences/io.podmandesktop.PodmanDesktop.plist
> * ~/Library/Preferences/ByHost/io.podmandesktop.PodmanDesktop.ShipIt*
> * ~/Library/Logs/Podman Desktop

@baurmatt, this issue is specifically for Podman not Podman Desktop. It looks like all the files you refer to are for Podman Desktop.

As for this issue, I'll try to take a look at implementing this soon.
Would be nice to have this for Linux too... trying to figure out how to get a completely fresh install on Ubuntu.

> Would be nice to have this for Linux too... trying to figure out how to get a completely fresh install on Ubuntu.

For Uninstall on Ubuntu, I remove the podman source from `apt` the package manager `rm -rf /etc/apt/sources.list.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:unstable.list`, and then `apt-get remove podman`. Source https://github.com/NiceNode/nice-node/blob/main/src/main/podman/uninstall/uninstallOnLinux.ts
That still leaves some files/directories behind... just counting ones with "podman" in the path I see `.config/containers/podman` `.local/share/containers/podman` perhaps there are others...

> That still leaves some files/directories behind... just counting ones with "podman" in the path I see `.config/containers/podman` `.local/share/containers/podman` perhaps there are others...

Thanks. Good catch. I'll check for all of them and update my script.
also found `.config/cni/net.d/87-podman.conflist`

Any word on this? Would be great to be able to [uninstall](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27026) in order to [re-install](https://github.com/orgs/Homebrew/discussions/6401#discussioncomment-14382400) :)
